acceptance_targets: [CH16_CRAFT_AFTERMATH_SPRAWL] Reading the revised passage from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:205 to the chapter end should show a clear sequence of new beats: Vess is gone, Aldric's silence registers as loss rather than relief, Aldric finally concedes she was right, and Maren's route toward the Woundmouth remains clear. Fail if more than one later paragraph after the departure beat merely rephrases the same idea of quiet/absence/world-indifference without adding new information, a changed relationship, or a decision.

[CH16_DIALOGUE_FAREWELL_NOTE_DERIVATION] In the revised farewell at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:193, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:197, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:201, Vess must still release Blackhall, mark Maren as genuinely capable, and reveal the missing sister-name wound. Fail if the passage still contains 'There should be a name here,' a close paraphrase of the style-bible sister-name calibration, or a tidy takeaway line that reads like advice or an aphorism rather than something Vess would say while coming apart.

[CH16_PROSE_ACOUSTIC_METAPHOR_TIC] In the revised prose after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:199, at most one paragraph may frame Vess's absence through sound/room/chord/music imagery. The surrounding paragraphs must communicate the same emotional fact through different sensory logic while preserving Maren's awareness that the entity has simplified and the skull is quieter.

[CH16_PROSE_VESS_PROFILE_CHECKLIST] In the revised lines anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:171 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:209, a reader should be able to point to at least one concrete Vess-specific behavior or missing reflex that is not just a category label. Fail if the passage still relies primarily on paired checklist phrasing such as exits/angles/distances and food/rooms/domestic inventory to explain who she was.
[R-05] Ch 16 contains no more than 2 occurrences of 'eleven years.'

findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised passage from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:205 to the chapter end should show a clear sequence of new beats: Vess is gone, Aldric's silence registers as loss rather than relief, Aldric finally concedes she was right, and Maren's route toward the Woundmouth remains clear. Fail if more than one later paragraph after the departure beat merely rephrases the same idea of quiet/absence/world-indifference without adding new information, a changed relationship, or a decision.
findings.problem: The chapter lands Vess's exit at line 205, then spends the rest of its length certifying the same loss in slightly different language. The bowl, the quiet, Maren's diminishment, and the world's indifference are all restated several times before the chapter ends. Aldric's delayed admission that she was right is the only major new turn in the back third, so the aftermath reads as overstatement and unearned chapter sprawl rather than accumulating consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the aftermath built around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:213; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:217; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:235; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:263; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:267; and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:269. Keep three beats only: Maren registering the absence after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:205, Aldric's weighted silence and eventual concession at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:237 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:249, and the forward vector toward the Woundmouth. Cut or replace paragraphs that only restate quiet, bowl-weight, or cosmic indifference without adding a new relational or tactical shift.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised farewell at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:193, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:197, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:201, Vess must still release Blackhall, mark Maren as genuinely capable, and reveal the missing sister-name wound. Fail if the passage still contains 'There should be a name here,' a close paraphrase of the style-bible sister-name calibration, or a tidy takeaway line that reads like advice or an aphorism rather than something Vess would say while coming apart.
findings.problem: Vess's goodbye slips into composed, note-derived dialogue at the exact emotional climax. 'You're bad at this. But you're real at it. That's the part that counts.' lands as a polished mini-speech rather than pressured speech, and 'There should be a name here' echoes the style-bible calibration around her missing sister-name closely enough to feel copied from reference material instead of discovered in scene. The result weakens voice integrity at the chapter's defining beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the farewell centered on snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:193; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:197; and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:201 so Vess stays clipped, damaged, and operational without delivering a quotable lesson or reusing style-bible phrasing. Keep the same dramatic functions: she releases the contract, she gives Maren one last assessment that sounds like her, and she exposes the sister-name absence in fresh language specific to this moment of dissipation.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised prose after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:199, at most one paragraph may frame Vess's absence through sound/room/chord/music imagery. The surrounding paragraphs must communicate the same emotional fact through different sensory logic while preserving Maren's awareness that the entity has simplified and the skull is quieter.
findings.problem: Once Vess leaves, the prose keeps translating loss through the same acoustic template: chord to single note, sound leaving a room, a room with three people, a room after furniture removal, the music stopping, the place where a sound had been. One such image would be strong; this many in one chapter turns the metaphor into autopilot and flattens the grief into a repeated construction.
findings.rewrite_direction: Choose one governing acoustic image at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:205 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:271, then revise the other passes at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:199; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:209; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:217; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:235; and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:251 so they render absence through materially different sensory evidence such as missing reflexes, body-state changes, or the sheep-pen environment.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised lines anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:171 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:209, a reader should be able to point to at least one concrete Vess-specific behavior or missing reflex that is not just a category label. Fail if the passage still relies primarily on paired checklist phrasing such as exits/angles/distances and food/rooms/domestic inventory to explain who she was.
findings.problem: At the moment Vess should feel most singular, the narration summarizes her through dossier-style trait lists: exits, approach angles, exact distances, what people ate, how rooms smelled, domestic inventory. Those details map so neatly onto her style-bible profile that the prose starts sounding like character notes recopied into the chapter instead of Maren grieving a specific person with specific habits.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the remembrance passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:171 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:209 so Vess's absence is rendered through one or two concrete remembered behaviors or missing reflexes from Maren's lived experience in this chapter, not through stacked category lists. Keep one tactical and one domestic loss if useful, but make them scene-level absences rather than profile headings.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 16 contains no more than 2 occurrences of 'eleven years.'
findings.evidence: The phrase 'eleven years' appears 6 times in Ch 16 (lines 4126-4398). Examples: L4252 bowl description; L4304 'In eleven years of fake rituals'; L4310 'waited eleven years to be useful'; L4352 'eleven years of lies and three weeks of something else.'
findings.problem: Ch 16 contains the emotional pivot where Vess dissipates and the bowl does real work for the first time. The scene's power comes from the consumption itself, not from restating 'eleven years' 6 times. By this point the phrase has appeared 76+ times. First established Ch 1 L9.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'eleven years' in Ch 16 from 6 to at most 2. Keep the strongest thematic contrast (L4352: 'eleven years of lies and three weeks of something else'). Cut or vary the rest.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
